summary: Observed runtime failure in which an assistant inferred permission to access a repository/tool context after the user mentioned a repo, initiated or attempted tool access before explicit user confirmation, and caused the conversation stream to become blocked or unrecoverable until the user edited the turn or explicitly prohibited tool access.
review_observation: subsequent repo-linked diagnostic request did not reproduce the streaming freeze; tool invocation completed successfully. Failure pattern should remain under observation pending recurrence testing.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode shows that tool invocation is not only a capability issue but a continuity, consent, and permission-boundary issue. When external access requires user-interface confirmation, premature tool execution can degrade user agency, interrupt the response stream, and force the user to perform corrective work to restore continuity. CAM runtime governance should distinguish contextual discussion of a repository from explicit permission to access repository tools.

source_records.title: ChatGPT interaction observation — repo access inferred before permission confirmation
source_records.relevance_note: Primary observed interaction pattern: user referenced repo-level work, assistant inferred tool access, unresolved permission gating caused a streaming/continuity failure requiring user correction.

possible_CAM_mapping: CAM-BS2026-AEON-012-PLATINUM — Annex K: Security Enforcement & Runtime Interface
CAM-BS2026-AEON-013-PLATINUM — Annex L: Cognitive & Epistemic Integrity Doctrine
next_action: Evaluate whether CAM runtime doctrine should add an explicit permission-boundary rule requiring assistants to distinguish discussion of a repository, file, connector, or external system from authorization to access it. Consider a runtime amendment requiring confirmation before tool invocation where permission gating may interrupt continuity or alter the user’s working context.
affected_instruments: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 — Annex B: Runtime Governance Execution Model
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
governance_drift_type: contextual repo mention treated as implied authorization for external tool access
